findings.name: animal_task.tr

line_hits.text: // PURPOSE:	Program used for carrying out scripted tasks for horses and other animals.
// Perform tasks while constraining the space if the task has an associated constraint.

strings_sample: This file contains TR programs and rules for animals performing tasks
Program used for carrying out scripted tasks for horses and other animals.
Not sure if we want to duplicate PerformTask or just use it,
but for now I created a copy, mostly to _prevent_ unsupported
script commands from working - I'm worried that otherwise
we would see horses patrolling around like guards and stuff
like that. We may be better off using PerformTask and instead
have preconditions that prevent undesired actions. /FF
// Perform tasks while constraining the space if the task has an associated constraint.
// This rule makes aiTaskTrActionBase-derived tasks work, which invoke
// some other TR program (or elementary action). /FF
// TASK_KILL_CHAR and TASK_MELEE_ATTACK are treated the same. This used to be running
// the human melee combat task program (PerformTaskMelee(x)), but now there is a dedicated
// attack program for animals. It includes searching for the target if it's not known. /FF

findings.name: human_unalerted.tr

line_hits.text: IsBumpingAllowed && Player(y) && !OnSameVehicleOrMountAsMe(y) && RidingOn(y, x) && BumpedIntoMeJustNow(x) -> SpeechEvent("BumpedIntoMe", y);
// continue a gringo conversation that is in progress - namely campfires!!!
//			constraint stuff normally done in PerformTask, we have to do it manually
// which takes care of at least most of the issues even if it's not all that elegant. /FF
//Execute lowest priority scripting commands (mostly intended to be used for the ambient ped default behavior).

strings_sample: // Standard operating procedure for guards who are not currently aware of an enemy presence.
// We have defeated (or forgotten?) all enemies.
((EventActive("PlayerBump", x) AND SpatialDistanceVeryClose(x) AND BumpedIntoMeVeryRecently(x)) OR
// If the player bumps us and we consider ourselves dangerous, stare him down for a little -JM
// Bumping should always be allowed unless disabled in script
// If I'm a horse and have a player riding me, and I've bumped somebody just now, play speech audio for it, since the above won't do it
IsBumpingAllowed && Player(y) && !OnSameVehicleOrMountAsMe(y) && RidingOn(y, x) && BumpedIntoMeJustNow(x) -> SpeechEvent("BumpedIntoMe", y);
// - This was moved to take precedence over the CaughtInGunfight program, which would otherwise
//   have a tendency to prevent it from working if there are multiple actors and some start shooting. /FF
// Act in a hostile fashion against x, if appropriate. The SpatialDistanceVeryClose(x)
// here is only to add a preference towards nearby targets, and isn't critical. /FF
// This "hostile behavior" was disabled, didn't work very well. See #166359: "Ped reactions to dishonorable players is really messy". /FF

// PURPOSE:	This is used as a part of a mechanism to ensure that the ambient population
// Remember being hit by players driving vehicles
// Dodge horse or vehicle about to hit us
// Brace for impact from horse or vehicle about to hit us
// only happens if the task wants it, as this is at a higher priority than carrying out
// running at highest priority. A rule further down takes care of the other case. /FF
// Riding around on same vehicle as bad guy is a bad idea, so get off
// If alerted vehicle driver is abandon fleeing, continue to abandon and flee

strings_sample: // If somebody hit us just now, make them the preferred target. This was originally added to
// make melee combat a little bit more interesting, consider taking it out if you suspect it's
// causing problems. On the other hand, we may actually want this to go beyond just melee damage. /FF
// Basic weapon pickup rules. Probably will need some polish. /FF
// If we are unarmed and can't pickup a nearby gun and enemy draws a gun, retreat
// This is here 3 times to give a preference to close targets, unless already attacked by others.
// Note that this relies on this being a TR program that's applied to all targets at once,
// the effect would be different if this was turned into a program EngageEnemy(x).
// Get on a horse if possible if an enemy is riding.
// Jog towards enemies that are far enough away to not be attacked by the CombatSearchAndAttack(x)
// above, but that nobody else is attacking. This (and the SpeedWalk version of the rule) takes
// 1. Get close enough to the actor for him to identify you as an enemy.
// 2. Move far away, but within vision range. Before, the actor would search,
//    now he should move towards you.
// Note that this assumes that the actor will be able to attack once he gets close.
// If others are already attacking, walk towards distant enemies.
// This program involves shooting at target x. If losing sight with the target
// the actor enters a quick search mode. If losing sight of the target for
// an extended period of time, it is intended that the user of this program

findings.name: human_guard.tr

line_hits.text: // InvestigateBody(x) takes care of finding out the status of a

strings_sample: // Behavior for guards who are attempting to detect an enemy presence.
// The rules here were adaped from what used to be an old GuardRoutine program
// which performed a number of things in parallel. This wasn't working well
// in practice, so I "inlined" everything here. The order of the rules here
// should roughly match the strength modifiers used in the old program, but
// some changes may be necessary as we go along. /FF
// Investigate suspicious noises in much the same way as explosions would be investigated. /FF
// -- Used to be in InvestigateAll (and InvestigateBodies, InvestigateExplosions, and InvestigateGunshots):
// If we encounter a corpse that died recently (but we didn't see it), go check out the body.
// - I took this out - until we hook up a proper animation, the body check just doesn't work
//   well, and since it's executed in parallel with other things, has a tendency to just cause
//   actors to crouch and be confused. /FF
// If we heard an explosion and we're looking at the location where we heard it,
// but we are still investigating it, then just keep looking.  No hurry!
EnableInvestigateAll && RecentExplosion(x) AND KnowsWhereIs(x) AND Investigating(x) && AllowedToInvestigate(x)
// If we heard an explosion and we're not finished investigating it,
// hurry over to look at the location where we heard the sound.
EnableInvestigateAll && RecentExplosion(x) AND NOT Investigated(x) && AllowedToInvestigate(x)
// If we hear someone unknown to us (or an enemy that we lost track of) firing a weapon,
// try going to the location where we heard the weapon.
// Identify unknowns with haste if enemies are known or believed to be present.
// If there are unknown persons around, identify them. This isn't done if

strings_sample: // Rules and programs for human reactions
// Note: BaseShouldMountedChargeReact(x, y) used to include !IsIndoors && !Riding && !RidingVehicle.
// This is now included where it's used from instead, for performance reasons - we branch on those
// early to bypass pairwise evaluation, and we don't reevaluate them if we do need the pairwise stuff.
// This is done as an optimization. /FF
// This was added as a necessary condition to evaluate BaseShouldMountedChargeReact(x, y)
// Need a concept of the direction to face based on last known position
// without requiring that the position was known recently.
// This is used for actors that flee away from some actor, then need to face the last known position
// Adding a condition for being indoors/outdoors when a given actor is indoors/outdoors, respectively.
// Only care if knifed a human (noone cares about chickens or horses)
// A person is considered in front of us if within a real large arc if close by
// or just a half circle if farther away.

findings.name: lookattarget.sc.xml

line_hits.text: <data name="delay_start_time" expr="arg0"></data><!--wait till we are behind the car-->

strings_sample: <!--arg0 = time to wait before lerping our current look vector towards the target-->
<!--arg1 = time to take lerping our current look vector towards the target-->
<!--arg2 = time when this transition is set to "complete"-->
<!--arg3 = offset your look at target by this much (meters down targets look vector)-->
<!--arg4 = rotate your up vector to this axis-->
<data name="delay_start_time" expr="arg0"></data><!--wait till we are behind the car-->
